title: Paddy's Has a Jumper

jokes.text: "If you'll have me as your husband, that is what you shall be." - "Whoa! What? Oh, my God. I did not see that coming."
jokes.setup: Characters are watching a period drama and reacting to plot developments
jokes.punchline: Their over-the-top investment in a cheesy romance show
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how these awful people get invested in trashy TV

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: If you'll have me as your husband, that is what you shall be.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Whoa! What? Oh, my God. I did not see that coming.

jokes.text: "I usually hate this old-time British crap... But, like, this-this show is amazing."
jokes.setup: Dennis claims to hate British period dramas
jokes.punchline: But admits he loves this one, showing his contradictory nature
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis hypocrisy - hates something until he likes it

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I usually hate this old-time British crap... But, like, this-this show is amazing.

jokes.text: "The algorithm... all of our previously-watched shows, they go into a data bank, so they can accurately predict which shows you'll love"
jokes.setup: Mac explaining how Netflix recommendations work
jokes.punchline: His overly technical explanation of basic algorithm function shows his pseudo-intellectualism
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's algorithm theme while showing Mac's character

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: The algorithm... all of our previously-watched shows, they go into a data bank, so they can accurately predict which shows you'll love

jokes.text: "Math and computers can think better for us than we can think for ourselves"
jokes.setup: Discussion about algorithms making decisions
jokes.punchline: Dennis's willingness to let technology do all his thinking
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's theme about removing human judgment

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Math and computers can think better for us than we can think for ourselves

jokes.text: "When you watch stuff like this, you feel like you want to eat like the British"
jokes.setup: Characters want fish and chips while watching British TV
jokes.punchline: The idea that watching period dramas makes you crave British food
jokes.explanation: Relatable observation about how media influences food cravings

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: When you watch stuff like this, you feel like you want to eat like the British

jokes.text: "Cop! Cop! I'm filming this! I didn't do it..."
jokes.setup: Police officer enters during a crisis
jokes.punchline: Dee immediately assumes she's in trouble and starts recording
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee reaction - paranoid and filming everything

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Cop! Cop! I'm filming this! I didn't do it...

jokes.text: "We have nothing to do with that!"
jokes.setup: Officer mentions a suicidal man on their roof
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate defensive denial of responsibility
jokes.explanation: Typical gang reaction - assume they'll be blamed for everything

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We have nothing to do with that!

jokes.text: "Thank you for your service. We love the police. Go blue. Blue Lives Matter."
jokes.setup: Officer tells them to stay put
jokes.punchline: Their over-the-top performative support for police
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing their fake patriotism when convenient

dialogue.line: Thank you for your service. We love the police. Go blue. Blue Lives Matter.

jokes.text: "We're not gonna get our fish and chips."
jokes.setup: They're trapped in the building during a suicide crisis
jokes.punchline: Charlie's only concern is missing their British food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie priority - food over human crisis

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We're not gonna get our fish and chips.

jokes.text: "Bryan O'Brien. I'd kill myself, too, if I had that clown's name."
jokes.setup: News reports the jumper's name
jokes.punchline: Dennis makes a cruel joke about the man's alliterative name
jokes.explanation: Dark but well-crafted joke showing Dennis's cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Bryan O'Brien. I'd kill myself, too, if I had that clown's name.

jokes.text: "You literally have the name of a clown."
jokes.setup: Dennis mocks Bryan O'Brien's name
jokes.punchline: Mac points out Dennis has an equally ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Good comeback that deflates Dennis's superiority

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You literally have the name of a clown.

jokes.text: "If the guy actually wanted to off himself, he'd hop into a warm bath and he'd do the old... north, south. Down the road instead of across the street."
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether the man will actually jump
jokes.punchline: Dee's detailed knowledge of suicide methods
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing Dee's suicidal ideation in casual conversation

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: If the guy actually wanted to off himself, he'd hop into a warm bath and he'd do the old... north, south. Down the road instead of across the street.

jokes.text: "The proper way to slit your wrist is take a knife and go from metacarpal straight to bicep, connecting with the artery the entire time."
jokes.setup: Continuing her suicide method explanation
jokes.punchline: Even more specific and disturbing technical details
jokes.explanation: Escalates the discomfort with medical precision

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: The proper way to slit your wrist is take a knife and go from metacarpal straight to bicep, connecting with the artery the entire time.

jokes.text: "I'm not talking... we're not talking about me. We're talking about him." "Are we not? I think we're..."
jokes.setup: Dee tries to deflect from her suicide knowledge
jokes.punchline: Mac's implication that they are indeed talking about her
jokes.explanation: Good character moment exposing Dee's obvious projection

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'm not talking... we're not talking about me. We're talking about him.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Are we not? I think we're...

jokes.text: "He's not gonna die falling from that height. Hard disagree, pal. You could absolutely die."
jokes.setup: Mac claims the fall isn't deadly
jokes.punchline: Dennis's aggressive disagreement about fall mortality
jokes.explanation: Sets up their absurd scientific debate

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: He's not gonna die falling from that height.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Hard disagree, pal. You could absolutely die.

jokes.text: "I am an American! I can believe in whatever I want in any given moment based on what argument I'm trying to make."
jokes.setup: Charlie challenges Mac's inconsistent beliefs about science
jokes.punchline: Mac's proud declaration of intellectual dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of intellectual inconsistency disguised as patriotism

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I am an American! I can believe in whatever I want in any given moment based on what argument I'm trying to make.

jokes.text: "You are not a true American, Charlie. I'm more American than you will ever be!"
jokes.setup: Mac's patriotic rant continues
jokes.punchline: Their absurd competition over who's more American
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their ridiculous argument

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You are not a true American, Charlie. I'm more American than you will ever be!

jokes.text: "What we need is an algorithm."
jokes.setup: The group is arguing emotionally
jokes.punchline: Dennis's solution is to use an algorithm for everything
jokes.explanation: Good callback that advances the episode's central premise

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What we need is an algorithm.

jokes.text: "My Behavioral Predictor algorithm... Could he? Would he? Should we?"
jokes.setup: Dennis presents his homemade algorithm chart
jokes.punchline: The overly simplified flowchart for a complex human situation
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Dennis's pseudo-intellectual approach

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: My Behavioral Predictor algorithm... Could he? Would he? Should we?

jokes.text: "Luckily I brought an egg to work today."
jokes.setup: They need something to test the fall with
jokes.punchline: Charlie just happens to have brought an egg
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie randomness - of course he brought an egg

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Luckily I brought an egg to work today.

jokes.text: "What would you say in this situation?" "I don't think he's sad, I think he's happy to get off this merry-go-round."
jokes.setup: Charlie asks what the jumper would say
jokes.punchline: Dee's immediately dark and personal response about wanting to die
jokes.explanation: Dark and revealing moment showing Dee's depression

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What would you say in this situation?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I don't think he's sad, I think he's happy to get off this merry-go-round.

jokes.text: "You had that in the chamber."
jokes.setup: Dee's immediate dark response
jokes.punchline: Mac pointing out how quickly she had that suicidal thought ready
jokes.explanation: Great observation about Dee's readiness with dark thoughts

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You had that in the chamber.

jokes.text: "I was hoping his entire skull would just explode. We all were, Mac."
jokes.setup: The egg didn't break dramatically
jokes.punchline: Their disappointment at lack of gore
jokes.explanation: Shows their bloodthirsty nature in casual conversation

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I was hoping his entire skull would just explode. We all were, Mac.

jokes.text: "It wasn't in a tree, it was in a hole. It's, like, a rat's egg or something."
jokes.setup: Further explaining where he got the egg
jokes.punchline: Charlie's complete ignorance of biology - rats don't lay eggs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie stupidity about basic animal facts

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It wasn't in a tree, it was in a hole. It's, like, a rat's egg or something.

jokes.text: "Frank's secret casaba melon... Why does Frank keep a casaba melon in the safe?"
jokes.setup: They need something to represent a head
jokes.punchline: Frank apparently keeps a melon in his safe for mysterious reasons
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag about Frank's mysterious melon

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Frank's secret casaba melon...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Why does Frank keep a casaba melon in the safe?

jokes.text: "Has he cut a hole in the rind, and then put it back on, like a plug?" "Oh, yeah."
jokes.setup: Wondering about Frank's melon
jokes.punchline: The obvious implication that Frank is having sex with it
jokes.explanation: Good buildup to the obvious but gross conclusion

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Has he cut a hole in the rind, and then put it back on, like a plug?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah.

jokes.text: "Stay away from my stuff. Don't put your hands near my casaba."
jokes.setup: They want to use Frank's melon
jokes.punchline: Frank's defensive reaction confirms their suspicions
jokes.explanation: Frank's possessiveness confirms the sexual nature

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Stay away from my stuff. Don't put your hands near my casaba.

jokes.text: "You don't understand. It's my son."
jokes.setup: Frank desperately wants to get inside during the crisis
jokes.punchline: Audience thinks he cares about the jumper, but he just wants his melon
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection making us think Frank cares about someone

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You don't understand. It's my son.

jokes.text: "Cricket! Where did you come from?" "I was up on the roof getting eggs from a nest."
jokes.setup: Cricket appears unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: He was stealing eggs too, explaining the missing egg from Charlie's nest
jokes.explanation: Good callback that explains the earlier egg situation

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Cricket! Where did you come from?

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: I was up on the roof getting eggs from a nest.

jokes.text: "I've fallen off buildings before; I never die. And look at me. I'm doing great."
jokes.setup: Cricket offers his experience falling off buildings
jokes.punchline: Claims he's 'doing great' while obviously being a disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Cricket is clearly not doing great at all

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: I've fallen off buildings before; I never die. And look at me. I'm doing great.

jokes.text: "You're sort of subhuman at this point."
jokes.setup: Cricket offers to be a control group
jokes.punchline: Dennis's casual dehumanization of Cricket
jokes.explanation: Typical Dennis cruelty delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're sort of subhuman at this point.

jokes.text: "Maureen Ponderosa. She died from falling off a much shorter building." "Yeah, but you pushed her."
jokes.setup: Dennis cites Maureen's death as data
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that Dennis murdered her
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Dennis's implied murder, treated casually

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Maureen Ponderosa. She died from falling off a much shorter building.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, but you pushed her.

jokes.text: "No, no, no, they never proved that. That was never even proven that she was pushed."
jokes.setup: Accused of pushing Maureen
jokes.punchline: Dennis's legalistic defense makes him sound more guilty
jokes.explanation: Dennis's defensive response makes him seem more guilty

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, no, no, they never proved that. That was never even proven that she was pushed.

jokes.text: "Just go through their trash. Sleep with them. Talk to their priest, then sleep with their priest. Then blackmail the priest."
jokes.setup: How to understand someone's psychology
jokes.punchline: Mac's increasingly elaborate and illegal scheme involving priests
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Mac's twisted Catholic mindset

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Just go through their trash. Sleep with them. Talk to their priest, then sleep with their priest. Then blackmail the priest.

jokes.text: "He's hardly a child, but he's still hanging on to his youth. That suggests daddy issues. His dad hates him as much as Mac's dad hates him."
jokes.setup: Dennis psychoanalyzing the jumper
jokes.punchline: Casually roasting Mac's daddy issues in the process
jokes.explanation: Good dig at Mac while pretending to analyze someone else

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: He's hardly a child, but he's still hanging on to his youth. That suggests daddy issues. His dad hates him as much as Mac's dad hates him.

jokes.text: "My dad loves me. My name's an homage." "It's an homage to a hamburger clown."
jokes.setup: Mac defends his father's love
jokes.punchline: Dennis pointing out Mac is named after Ronald McDonald
jokes.explanation: Excellent burn revealing the sad truth about Mac's name

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: My dad loves me. My name's an homage.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's an homage to a hamburger clown.

jokes.text: "Butt-eating... Where are you getting that from?" "There's a certain glint in the eye, you know, a sparkle."
jokes.setup: Dennis claims the man in photos is into butt-eating
jokes.punchline: His explanation that you can see it in someone's eyes
jokes.explanation: Absurd Dennis logic about detecting sexual preferences

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Butt-eating... Where are you getting that from?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: There's a certain glint in the eye, you know, a sparkle.

jokes.text: "You can see that in someone's eye?" "Have you been eating butts? Of course she has!"
jokes.setup: Dee questions if you can see butt-eating in eyes
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately assumes she's asking from experience
jokes.explanation: Standard Dee roast but well-executed

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You can see that in someone's eye?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Have you been eating butts? Of course she has!

jokes.text: "That Dee's a nasty fucking slut." "Definitely, definitely, but also that the poorly named Bryan O'Brien most definitely would kill himself."
jokes.setup: Dennis asks what they learned from their algorithm
jokes.punchline: Mac focuses on insulting Dee, Dennis pivots back to the suicide
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing their twisted priorities

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That Dee's a nasty fucking slut.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Definitely, definitely, but also that the poorly named Bryan O'Brien most definitely would kill himself.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, he's a real deranged person. This kid's a real whack job. I'm afraid he's gonna do something really stupid this time.
jokes.setup: Police ask if Bryan has exhibited this behavior before
jokes.punchline: Frank (who isn't the father) confidently describes him as crazy
jokes.explanation: Frank continuing his fake father charade with improvised backstory

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah, he's a real deranged person. This kid's a real whack job. I'm afraid he's gonna do something really stupid this time.

jokes.text: "My son is actually a big fan of yours. It would really get under his skin if he knew I was standing next to those. You. You."
jokes.setup: Frank talking to news reporter about his 'son'
jokes.punchline: His obvious fixation on her breasts, catching himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank perverted moment with attempted recovery

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: My son is actually a big fan of yours. It would really get under his skin if he knew I was standing next to those. You. You.

jokes.text: "We could become like a hot spot for jumpers. Turn them into martyrs. Highway to Hell, Free Fallin'."
jokes.setup: Discussing turning the bar into a suicide theme
jokes.punchline: Their enthusiastic embrace of profiting from death
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing their complete lack of humanity

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We could become like a hot spot for jumpers. Turn them into martyrs. Highway to Hell, Free Fallin'.

jokes.text: "The algorithm is telling us that this guy could die, wants to die, and that we should help him do it. That's just math."
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining their algorithmic conclusion
jokes.punchline: Using 'math' to justify murder
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of how people use 'objectivity' to justify horrible acts

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The algorithm is telling us that this guy could die, wants to die, and that we should help him do it. That's just math.

jokes.text: "Feelings just get in the way."
jokes.setup: Mac processing their murder plan
jokes.punchline: Dismissing empathy as inconvenient
jokes.explanation: Chilling casual dismissal of human emotion

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Feelings just get in the way.

jokes.text: "I'll push him. I don't give a shit."
jokes.setup: Discussion of how to help the jumper die
jokes.punchline: Cricket's immediate willingness to commit murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cricket - so broken he'll do anything

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: I'll push him. I don't give a shit.

jokes.text: "If I want to bang it, I'm gonna bang it. Not that I am banging it, but I could. And if it doesn't splatter to bits, I'm gonna bang it later."
jokes.setup: Frank on live TV talking to his 'son'
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing his melon obsession on television
jokes.explanation: Good payoff of Frank accidentally exposing his perversion

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: If I want to bang it, I'm gonna bang it. Not that I am banging it, but I could. And if it doesn't splatter to bits, I'm gonna bang it later.

jokes.text: "He's calling me strange?"
jokes.setup: News reports about estranged father
jokes.punchline: Frank mishears 'estranged' as 'strange'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that fits Frank's character

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: He's calling me strange?

jokes.text: "Let's try to think of it more analytically, more mathematically. It's sort of like a deletion. Like hitting Control-Alt-Delete. Bloop. He's in the trash."
jokes.setup: Gang wants to avoid calling it murder
jokes.punchline: Dennis's computer metaphor for human death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dehumanization through tech metaphors

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Let's try to think of it more analytically, more mathematically. It's sort of like a deletion. Like hitting Control-Alt-Delete. Bloop. He's in the trash.

jokes.text: "What a gyp. We were gonna... we had a good plan."
jokes.setup: Jumper comes down safely
jokes.punchline: Dennis's disappointment at not getting to commit murder
jokes.explanation: Casually disappointed about missing a murder opportunity

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What a gyp. We were gonna... we had a good plan.

jokes.text: "This isn't my dad." "Not of this sad sack. I got distracted by the reporter's bagonzas."
jokes.setup: Jumper meets his supposed father
jokes.punchline: Frank reveals he lied just to meet the attractive reporter
jokes.explanation: Good payoff revealing Frank's selfish motivation

dialogue.speaker: Bryan
dialogue.line: This isn't my dad.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Not of this sad sack. I got distracted by the reporter's bagonzas.

jokes.text: "It's where I stash my Maui Kush."
jokes.setup: Gang finally learns why Frank loves his melon
jokes.punchline: It's just a drug hiding spot, not sexual
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection payoff - less gross than expected

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It's where I stash my Maui Kush.

jokes.text: "Pot's pretty much legal now. You don't really have to stash it anywhere."
jokes.setup: Frank explains his elaborate drug hiding
jokes.punchline: Mac points out it's unnecessary in current times
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about changing drug laws

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Pot's pretty much legal now. You don't really have to stash it anywhere.

jokes.text: I wouldn't eat it, Cricks. It's full of loads.
jokes.setup: Cricket about to eat Frank's casaba melon
jokes.punchline: Dennis warns him it's full of Frank's semen
jokes.explanation: Gross-out ending callback, economical but somewhat cheap

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I wouldn't eat it, Cricks. It's full of loads.

jokes.text: "Mathematically we were supposed to kill a man today. Maybe that's part of the problem of taking the humanity out of decisions."
jokes.setup: Realizing the algorithm made them watch a show they hate
jokes.punchline: Dennis connects this to their murder plot, showing brief self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Excellent thematic conclusion connecting TV algorithms to their murderous logic

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Mathematically we were supposed to kill a man today. Maybe that's part of the problem of taking the humanity out of decisions.

jokes.text: "It's full of loads."
jokes.setup: Cricket about to eat the casaba melon
jokes.punchline: Frank reveals it is sexual after all - his euphemism for semen
jokes.explanation: Final gross-out payoff confirming their original suspicions

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It's full of loads.

tmdb_overview: Trapped inside Paddy's with a suicide jumper on the roof, the Gang wonders if they should bother intervening with an algorithm of their own - Could he, would he, should we? the second half hour, Dennis and Charlie stand guard at the entrance to their laser tag base waiting to ambush the legendary "Big Mo," but Dennis begins to ponder the reasons they play laser tag and if their pursuit of a victory is worth their effort.
